title: 10 best herbal creams for viral hepatitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with viral hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are hallmark symptoms of the disease. Additionally, dandelion's bitter compounds may help stimulate the body's natural detoxification processes, allowing it to more effectively clear viral particles from the liver and restore healthy function. This natural approach can provide relief from the discomfort and fatigue associated with viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, can reduce liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis, alleviating symptoms like pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's high antioxidant content helps protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate liver disease progression in patients with viral hepatitis.

Choleretic properties: Dandelion root's choleretic effect, triggered by the presence of inulin and other saponins, can stimulate bile production and flow, helping to eliminate toxins and reduce liver congestion associated with viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, these immature leaves help combat liver damage by promoting regeneration of healthy liver cells and reducing fibrosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of dried taraxacum officinale herb with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 4 hours or overnight at room temperature to allow for infusing and extracting properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil for use in cream preparation.

Mix the infused oil with 200 grams of beeswax, 100 milliliters of distilled water, and 20 grams of vitamin e oil, heating the mixture in a double boiler until melted and cooled to a consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with viral hepatitis because they contain silymarin, a bioflavonoid compound that has potent antioxidant properties. Silymarin helps to protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and toxins, promoting healthy liver function and reducing inflammation. Additionally, milk thistle may help to stimulate the production of glutathione, an important antioxidant enzyme that aids in the detoxification process, thereby supporting the body's natural defense against viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, silymarin has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses such as hepatitis C and HIV by binding to viral RNA and preventing its translation into proteins.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle cream, including silymarin and other compounds, have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can occur as a result of hepatitis infection.

Saponins: Saponins, another class of compounds found in milk thistle, have been shown to increase the production of glutathione, an essential antioxidant enzyme in the liver that helps protect against damage caused by free radicals and toxins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to protect liver cells from damage caused by viral hepatitis.
Seeds: Contain high levels of silybin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound that helps reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum leaves and flowers in their optimal growing conditions for at least 30 days.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or low-temperature drying equipment within 24 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder within 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 1/4 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2 within 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 weeks in a dark glass jar, shaking daily, before straining and bottling the cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with viral hepatitis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have antiviral properties. Glycyrrhizin can inhibit the replication of the virus, reducing its ability to cause damage to the liver and alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain, and digestive issues. Additionally, licorice creams may also help to reduce inflammation in the liver, promoting a faster recovery from viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which can help reduce liver damage and inflammation caused by viral hepatitis.

Isomaltose: This sugar substitute has been shown to inhibit the replication of viral particles, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms and improving treatment outcomes for viral hepatitis patients.
Phenolic compounds: These antioxidant-rich compounds have been found to scavenge free radicals and protect liver cells from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes contain a unique combination of compounds like isoflavones and triterpenoids, which exhibit antiviral activity against various viruses, including those responsible for viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder in 300 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Combine the extracted solution with 100 milliliters of vegetable glycerin and stir until dissolved at room temperature.
Mix the resulting liquid with 200 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax, then heat the mixture to 70 degrees celsius for 15 minutes.
Allow the heated mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer at room temperature.
Pour the finished cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 2-8 degrees celsius for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with viral hepatitis because of its ability to target and destroy the virus that causes it. Additionally, wormwood's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce liver damage and promote healing. By addressing the underlying viral infection and supporting liver function, wormwood creams may help alleviate symptoms and improve treatment outcomes for patients with viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Therapeutic Antiviral Activity: Wormwood's antiviral compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viral hepatitis B and C, reducing inflammation and liver damage.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The antioxidants present in wormwood creams, including polyphenols and phenolic acids, help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can exacerbate hepatitis symptoms and promote liver scarring.

Immune System Modulation: Wormwood's immunomodulatory compounds may help regulate the body's immune response to viral hepatitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and preventing chronic liver disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of essential oils, including camphor and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have antiviral properties effective against viral hepatitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems then dry them in the shade for 14 days.
Grind 20g of dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 3 minutes.
Mix the powdered artemisia absinthium with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of beeswax pellets in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat on the stovetop or in the microwave until the beeswax melts completely after 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into a clean glass container and let it cool at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin at a ratio of 1:2 for 24 hours.
Combine the extracted liquid with beeswax and shea butter in a ratio of 3:2:1, heating it to melt at 100°f for 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and jojoba oil to the mixture, stirring well before pouring into sterilized containers and cooling down to room temperature.
Allow the cream to mature for 7 days, shaking the container daily, before filtering it through cheesecloth and transferring to airtight jars.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba cream at room temperature for up to 6 months, using it as needed for relief from viral hepatitis symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Grind dried viscum album bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.

Add 10ml of olive oil and stir until melted and well combined, taking approximately 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 60°c before whipping with a hand mixer for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and seal tightly, allowing it to set at room temperature for 24 hours.

Heat the mixture gently stirring constantly over low heat for 15 minutes until the ingredients are fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems, ensuring no signs of contamination or disease, in sufficient quantity for the desired cream yield.
Dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator or air circulation at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content, resulting in a dry herb weight ratio of 1:3.
Grind the dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 20 grams of powdered herb per 100 grams of dried plant material.

Mix the powdered herb with 300 milliliters of carrier oil, such as sweet almond or jojoba oil, in a glass jar at a ratio of 1 part achillea millefolium to 3 parts oil, allowing for a 24-hour maceration period.

Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer it into sterilized cream jars, allowing the infused cream to mature for 48 hours before use, with a shelf life of up to 6 months when stored in an airtight container at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for viral hepatitis are topical preparations made from natural ingredients that have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties. These creams help to soothe and calm the skin affected by viral hepatitis, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Examples of herbal creams that aid in viral hepatitis treatment include those containing aloe vera, tea tree oil, and turmeric, which have been shown to reduce symptoms such as itching, redness, and scaling. By providing relief from these uncomfortable symptoms, herbal creams improve the quality of life for individuals living with viral hepatitis, allowing them to manage their condition more effectively and maintain a better sense of overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat viral hepatitis with herbal creams is by using a combination of herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory properties. Turmeric, ginger, and milk thistle are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and improve liver function. Silymarin, a flavonoid extracted from milk thistle, has been shown to prevent liver damage and promote liver regeneration. These herbs can be used topically as creams or ointments to provide targeted relief to the affected area.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to viral hepatitis that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing symptoms of redness, itching, and inflammation. These natural remedies can provide relief from skin irritation and promote healthy skin regeneration without harsh chemicals or side effects.
